definitions: One should not make judgments about the worth or character of someone or something based solely on outward appearance.
External appearances can be misleading and do not always reflect the true nature of a person or thing.
examples: She seemed quiet at first, but you can't judge a book by its cover; she's actually very outgoing.
The restaurant looked a bit run-down, but the food was amazing. You really can't judge a book by its cover.
Don't dismiss the old car just because of its age; you can't judge a book by its cover, it might run perfectly.
lemma: can't judge a book by its cover
source: can't judge a book by its cover
